methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $260M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Chroma Medicine is a Series B-stage biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector that has raised $260M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private peers at the same stage and 3 public sector benchmarks.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses total raised ($260M) as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-later advancement, yielding $494M. Peer-median anchoring across 5 same-stage, same-sector companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—establishes a $810M median with a $600M–$3.3B range. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (6.6x median across RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) cannot be applied without the target's revenue figures.

The triangulated range is $420M–$931M, with a median of $652M. This reflects meaningful dispersion between the conservative stage-progression floor and the peer-median ceiling, reflecting heterogeneity within the Series B biotech cohort. Key uncertainties include the company's clinical-stage progression, intellectual property scope, and competitive positioning within the five-peer reference set.
